content.text: Good! Now I can see the files. Let me read them:

content.content:
1→#!/usr/bin/env node
     2→/**
     3→ * Spawn command for creating new agents in iTerm2
     4→ */
     5→
     6→import { spawnSync } from 'node:child_process'
     7→import { existsSync } from 'node:fs'
     8→import { join, dirname, resolve } from 'node:path'
     9→import { fileURLToPath } from 'node:url'
    10→import chalk from 'chalk'
    11→import { Command } from 'commander'
    12→import { loadConfig } from '../config/loader.js'
    13→
    14→const __filename = fileURLToPath(import.meta.url)
    15→const __dirname = dirname(__filename)
    16→
    17→interface SpawnOptions {
    18→  name?: string
    19→  vertical?: boolean
    20→  args?: string
    21→  noLabel?: boolean
    22→}
    23→
    24→function findITermScriptsDir(): string | null {
    25→  // Look for iterm_scripts directory in multiple locations
    26→  const possiblePaths = [
    27→    // Relative to CLI dist directory
    28→    join(__dirname, '..', '..', '..', '..', 'iterm_scripts'),
    29→    // Relative to project root
    30→    join(process.cwd(), 'iterm_scripts'),
    31→    // In .crewchief directory
    32→    join(process.cwd(), '.crewchief', 'iterm_scripts'),
    33→    // Absolute path in home directory
    34→    join(process.env.HOME || '', '.crewchief', 'iterm_scripts'),
    35→  ]
    36→
    37→  for (const path of possiblePaths) {
    38→    const scriptPath = join(path, 'spawn_agent.py')
    39→    if (existsSync(scriptPath)) {
    40→      return path
    41→    }
    42→  }
    43→
    44→  return null
    45→}
    46→
    47→function detectTerminalBackend(): 'iterm' | null {
    48→  // Check if we're in iTerm2
    49→  if (process.env.TERM_PROGRAM === 'iTerm.app') {
    50→    return 'iterm'
    51→  }
    52→
    53→  return null
    54→}
    55→
    56→function resolveRepoRoot(startDir: string): string {
    57→  try {
    58→    const res = spawnSync('git', ['rev-parse', '--git-common-dir'], {
    59→      cwd: startDir,
    60→      encoding: 'utf-8',
    61→    })
    62→    if (res.status === 0) {
    63→      const commonDir = res.stdout.trim()
    64→      const commonAbs = resolve(startDir, commonDir)
    65→      // repo root is parent of the common .git directory
    66→      return dirname(commonAbs)
    67→    }
    68→  } catch {}
    69→  return startDir
    70→}
    71→
    72→export function registerSpawnCommand(program: Command): void {
    73→  program
    74→    .command('spawn')
    75→    .description('Spawn AI agent(s) in dedicated terminal pane(s) with their own worktrees')
    76→    .argument('<agents>', 'Agent type(s) - single or comma-separated (e.g., claude or claude,gemini,codex)')
    77→    .argument('[task]', 'Optional task description to include in agent name(s)')
    78→    .option('-n, --name <name>', 'Custom name for the agent')
    79→    .option('-v, --vertical', 'Split pane vertically instead of horizontally')
    80→    .option('-a, --args <args>', 'Additional arguments to pass to the agent command')
    81→    .option('--no-label', 'Skip labeling the pane')
    82→    .option('--backend <backend>', 'Force specific backend (iterm only)')
    83→    .action(async (agent: string, task: string | undefined, options: SpawnOptions) => {
    84→      try {
    85→        const config = await loadConfig()
    86→
    87→        // Detect or use specified backend
    88→        const backend = options.backend || config.terminal?.backend || 'auto'
    89→        const detectedBackend = backend === 'auto' ? detectTerminalBackend() : backend
    90→
    91→        if (!detectedBackend) {
    92→          console.error(chalk.red('❌ No suitable terminal backend found'))
    93→          console.error(chalk.yellow('   Please install iTerm2 (macOS)'))
    94→          console.error(chalk.dim('   Download from: https://iterm2.com/downloads.html'))
    95→          process.exit(1)
    96→        }
    97→
    98→        if (detectedBackend === 'iterm') {
    99→          // Find iterm_scripts directory
   100→          const scriptsDir = findITermScriptsDir()
   101→          if (!scriptsDir) {
   102→            console.error(chalk.red('❌ Could not find iterm_scripts directory'))
   103→            console.error(chalk.yellow('   Make sure iTerm2 scripts are installed'))
   104→            process.exit(1)
   105→          }
   106→
   107→          // Check if spawning multiple agents (comma-separated)
   108→          const isMultiAgent = agent.includes(',')
   109→
   110→          let spawnScript: string
   111→          if (isMultiAgent && existsSync(join(scriptsDir, 'spawn_multi_agents.py'))) {
   112→            // Use multi-agent spawning script
   113→            spawnScript = join(scriptsDir, 'spawn_multi_agents.py')
   114→          } else if (!isMultiAgent && existsSync(join(scriptsDir, 'spawn_agent_smart.py'))) {
   115→            // Use smart spawning with intelligent pane management
   116→            spawnScript = join(scriptsDir, 'spawn_agent_smart.py')
   117→          } else {
   118→            // Fallback to basic spawn
   119→            spawnScript = join(scriptsDir, 'spawn_agent.py')
   120→          }
   121→
   122→          // Resolve repository root to ensure worktrees are under repo-level .crewchief/worktrees
   123→          const projectDir = resolveRepoRoot(process.cwd())
   124→
   125→          // Build spawn command arguments
   126→          let args: string[]
   127→
   128→          if (isMultiAgent) {
   129→            // Multi-agent spawning
   130→            console.log(chalk.blue('🤖 Spawning multiple agents...'))
   131→
   132→            // Parse agent types (comma-separated)
   133→            const agentTypes = agent
   134→              .split(',')
   135→              .map((a) => a.trim())
   136→              .filter(Boolean)
   137→            console.log(chalk.dim(`   Agents: ${agentTypes.join(', ')}`))
   138→
   139→            // For multi-agent, pass agents and task directly
   140→            args = [spawnScript, agent]
   141→
   142→            if (task) {
   143→              args.push(task)
   144→            }
   145→
   146→            args.push('--project-dir', projectDir)
   147→
   148→            if (options.args) {
   149→              args.push('--args', options.args)
   150→            }
   151→          } else {
   152→            // Single agent spawning
   153→            // Generate agent name in format: {name}__{agent}
   154→            let baseName: string
   155→            if (options.name) {
   156→              // Use provided name
   157→              baseName = options.name
   158→            } else if (task) {
   159→              // Use task as the name
   160→              baseName = task.replace(/\s+/g, '-').toLowerCase()
   161→            } else {
   162→              // Generate a simple name with timestamp
   163→              const timestamp = new Date().toISOString().replace(/[:.]/g, '-').slice(0, 19)
   164→              baseName = `${agent}-${timestamp}`
   165→            }
   166→
   167→            // Create the base worktree name
   168→            let agentName = `${baseName}__${agent}`
   169→
   170→            // Check if worktree already exists at repo root
   171→            const worktreePath = join(projectDir, '.crewchief', 'worktrees', agentName)
   172→            if (existsSync(worktreePath)) {
   173→              // Append timestamp if worktree exists
   174→              const timestamp = new Date().toISOString().replace(/[:.]/g, '-').slice(0, 19)
   175→              agentName = `${baseName}__${agent}_${timestamp}`
   176→              console.log(chalk.yellow(`⚠️  Worktree ${baseName}__${agent} exists, using ${agentName}`))
   177→            }
   178→
   179→            args = [spawnScript, agent, '--name', agentName, '--project-dir', projectDir]
   180→
   181→            if (options.vertical) {
   182→              args.push('--vertical')
   183→            }
   184→
   185→            if (options.args) {
   186→              args.push('--args', options.args)
   187→            }
   188→          }
   189→
   190→          if (options.noLabel) {
   191→            args.push('--no-label')
   192→          }
   193→
   194→          console.log(chalk.cyan('🚀 Spawning agent via iTerm2...'))
   195→          console.log(chalk.dim(`   Script: ${spawnScript}`))
   196→          console.log(chalk.dim(`   Agent: ${agent}`))
   197→          console.log(chalk.dim(`   Project: ${projectDir}`))
   198→
   199→          // Execute the spawn script
   200→          const result = spawnSync('python3', args, {
   201→            stdio: 'inherit',
   202→            encoding: 'utf-8',
   203→          })
   204→
   205→          if (result.status !== 0) {
   206→            console.error(chalk.red('❌ Failed to spawn agent'))
   207→            if (result.error) {
   208→              console.error(chalk.dim(`   Error: ${result.error.message}`))
   209→            }
   210→            process.exit(1)
   211→          }
   212→
   213→          if (isMultiAgent) {
   214→            console.log(chalk.green('✅ Agents spawned successfully'))
   215→            console.log(chalk.dim("   Use 'crewchief agent list' to see all agents"))
   216→            console.log(chalk.dim("   Use 'crewchief agent message <agent-name> <text>' to send commands"))
   217→          } else {
   218→            console.log(chalk.green('✅ Agent spawned successfully'))
   219→            console.log(chalk.dim("   Use 'crewchief agent list' to see all agents"))
   220→            // Only show specific agent name for single spawn
   221→            const agentName = args.find((a, i) => args[i - 1] === '--name')
   222→            if (agentName) {
   223→              console.log(chalk.dim(`   Use 'crewchief agent message ${agentName} <text>' to send commands`))
   224→            }
   225→          }
   226→        }
   227→      } catch (error) {
   228→        console.error(chalk.red('❌ Error spawning agent:'), error)
   229→        process.exit(1)
   230→      }
   231→    })
   232→    .addHelpText(
   233→      'after',
   234→      `
   235→Examples:
   236→  Single agent:
   237→  $ crewchief spawn claude                    # Spawn Claude with auto-generated name
   238→  $ crewchief spawn claude "auth-feature"     # Include task in name
   239→  $ crewchief spawn claude --name my-agent    # Use custom name
   240→  
   241→  Multiple agents:
   242→  $ crewchief spawn claude,gemini implement-auth     # Spawn both Claude and Gemini
   243→  $ crewchief spawn claude,gemini,codex code-review  # Spawn three agents
   244→  $ crewchief spawn "claude, gemini" fix-bug         # With spaces (quoted)
   245→  
   246→  With options:
   247→  $ crewchief spawn claude --vertical         # Split vertically (single agent only)
   248→
   249→Supported agents:
   250→  - claude    Anthropic's Claude (uses 'claude' command)
   251→  - gemini    Google's Gemini (uses 'gemini' command)
   252→  - codex     OpenAI Codex (uses 'codex' command)
   253→  - cursor    Cursor AI (uses 'cursor-agent' command)
   254→  - aider     Aider coding assistant (uses 'aider' command)
   255→  - custom    Any custom command
   256→
   257→Spawning behavior:
   258→  Single agent:
   259→    - Creates one new pane with intelligent splitting
   260→    - One git worktree: .crewchief/worktrees/<task>__<agent>
   261→  
   262→  Multiple agents:
   263→    - Creates multiple panes with hierarchical layout
   264→    - Separate worktrees for each: <task>__<agent1>, <task>__<agent2>, etc.
   265→    - First agent: vertical split (left/right)
   266→    - Additional agents: horizontal splits of right pane`,
   267→    )
   268→}
   269→

<system-reminder>
Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.
</system-reminder>
